growth_records.cite: Dey et al. Density-dependent feedback and higher-order interactions enable coexistence in phage-bacteria community dynamics. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag041. [PMC13077301]
growth_records.snippet: We measured the growth rate r through doubling time experiments, finding that CBA strains divided every 4.2-5.8 h and PSA strains divided every 3.6-3.8 h (Fig. 2c).
growth_records.notes: Doubling times reported directly in text for the pooled group of 2 Pseudoalteromonas (PSA) strains, measured in phage-free control flasks (200 mL PC medium in 2 L flasks, ~2x10^9 initial cells, sampled by OD600/CFU every 35 min for 15h45m; strains routinely cultured at room temperature per Methods 2.1, exact numeric temperature not stated so left null). mu = ln2/doubling_time: for td=3.6h, mu=0.693/3.6=0.193 h^-1; for td=3.8h, mu=0.693/3.8=0.182 h^-1 -> range ~0.182-0.193 h^-1. Range aggregated across the 2 PSA strains (H100, 13-15), not resolved individually in the text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, oxygen]

growth_records.medium: Marine Basal Medium + 3 mM ammonium + phytoplankton metabolite (12 mM carbon)

up.units: growth substrate (only strain with dual signal+substrate role on spermidine); numeric in Dataset S1

growth_records.cite: Clerc et al. Chemotaxis, growth, and inter-species interactions shape early bacterial community assembly. ISME J (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf101. [PMC12202767]
growth_records.snippet: spermidine displayed a dual role (signal and substrate) only for Pseudoalteromonas sp. HF66 ... the only strain that exhibited chemotaxis and growth on both DMSP and spermidine
growth_records.notes: No numeric growth rate in text; values in Dataset S1/S3 and Figs 1/4. Text confirms HF66 grows on both DMSP and spermidine as substrates. Medium base not itemized -> stated=false. No arithmetic performed.
